w:r.w:t: Dreamland Travels Project: Interview and Viva Explanation
This project is a full-stack travel booking web application built with Django.
Its main purpose is to let users:
? filter packages by type, budget, and duration,
? pay online through Razorpay or choose offline cash payment,
? and let admins manage packages, bookings, and feedback.
In simple words, this project digitizes the workflow of a travel agency.
If I have to explain it in one line to an interviewer:
Dreamland Travels is a Django-based travel booking management system with user booking, payment integration, admin dashboards, feedback handling, invoice generation, and optional REST APIs.
The goal of the project is to solve common manual travel-agency problems:
? customer feedback is not centrally stored.
This project creates one platform where:
? users can discover and book trips,
? admins can manage travel inventory and booking review,
? and all operational data stays organized.
The project follows the classic Django architecture:
1. Browser sends request to a URL.
2. URL routes request to a Django view function.
4. View uses models to read/write database.
5. View passes data to templates for rendering HTML.
6. Some features also expose REST API responses via serializers and API views.
? and API endpoints for programmatic access.
This is the Django command-line entry point.
? Django needs one launcher file to know which settings module to load.
? so the project can be recreated in another machine or server.
This provides sample environment variables for:
? sensitive data should not be hardcoded in source code.
This is the local development database.
? SQLite is lightweight and easy for local testing and development.
These help deployment platforms run the project.
? to install dependencies, collect static files, and start Gunicorn.
This folder contains global project configuration.
This is the most important configuration file.
Used to build file paths relative to the project root.
This function manually loads environment variables from `.env`.
? works even without external env-loading libraries.
? Django only activates features for apps listed here.
The project checks whether DRF is installed using `find_spec`.
? makes the project robust even if DRF is missing.
? one codebase supports both development and deployment.
? separates static assets from templates and Python code.
Used for uploaded files like package images.
? uploaded content should be stored separately from code files.
? registration and booking confirmation emails must be sent.
? payment gateway integration needs keys and branding.
This is the root URL router.
These are standard Django server entry points.
? `wsgi.py` is used by traditional servers like Gunicorn,
This app contains the actual business logic of the project.
? likely used for a simple text/content model.
? currently it is not central to booking flow.
? stores detailed registration information beyond Django’s built-in `User`.
? this custom table stores business-specific user profile data.
? this model stores password in plain text, which is not secure.
? For production, password should never be stored separately in plain form.
? Only Django’s hashed password system should be used.
This is a genuine improvement point you can mention honestly.
? feedback helps admins analyze user satisfaction and discover how users found the platform.
? stores all travel package details shown to users.
? this is the main product catalog of the travel website.
? package may have either uploaded image or external image URL.
? if uploaded file matches a static image name, it can resolve to static asset.
? when package image is updated, old file should be deleted.
? when package is deleted, corresponding uploaded image file should also be removed.
This is the central transaction model of the project.
? stores one booking made by one user for one package.
? it links user, package, admin review, payment state, and booking timeline in one place.
? admin workflow needs clear state management.
? payment lifecycle needs to be tracked correctly.
? a user can only keep one active booking row per package.
? if booking is updated, same row is reused instead of duplicate rows.
? multiplies package price by traveler count.
? some old rows may still show pending even though Razorpay payment evidence exists.
? this property corrects stale pending data by checking `paid_at` or `razorpay_payment_id`.
? gives user-friendly status label based on resolved status.
Forms are used for validation and HTML form generation.
? admin uses this form to create or update packages.
? if external image URL is used, old uploaded image is removed.
? prevents invalid feedback data from being saved.
? requires traveler count, date, contact number, payment method,
? limits payment method choices shown in the form,
? travel date cannot be earlier than today.
? business rules must be enforced before booking is saved.
? admin form used while reviewing a booking.
? approval should carry operational notes, not just yes/no action.
Serializers are used for API input/output.
Used in package listing and package detail APIs.
? exposes feedback fields in structured JSON form.
? API registration needs both auth account and user profile data.
? validates presence of email and password.
? gives authenticated user a booking history response in JSON.
This file provides REST API endpoints.
? same filtering logic is useful in APIs too.
? returns one active package by id.
? returns a clean JSON success response with user info.
? enables frontend or external clients to login programmatically.
? returns bookings only for current logged-in user.
This file contains the core business logic.
? prevents payment flow from running without credentials.
? Razorpay expects amount in smallest currency unit.
Creates order on Razorpay using HTTP request.
? online payment checkout needs a valid Razorpay order id.
? ensures payment data is genuine and untampered.
? only trusted webhook events should update payments.
Central helper to update booking payment fields.
? avoids repeating payment update logic in multiple places.
Returns the best display name for the user.
? both invoice pages need formatted invoice labels.
Sends booking email after booking create/update.
? separates authentication account creation from extended profile storage.
? shows invalid credential error if needed.
? admin and customer experiences are intentionally separated.
If normal user is logged in:
? feedback should come from actual users, not staff.
? returns JSON for AJAX or redirects with messages for regular form submit.
? same endpoint supports both dynamic and normal submission flow.
? helps users narrow options instead of browsing everything blindly.
? creates filter options dynamically from available packages,
? shows only filtered packages when filters are active,
? marks packages already booked by user.
? excludes cancelled bookings from active user booking display.
? package listing must be aware of user’s booking state.
Shows full details of one package.
? prepares Razorpay checkout context if payment is pending and order exists.
? this page is both information page and booking control page.
This is one of the most important functions.
? resets cancelled booking if rebooking happens,
? handles non-Razorpay and Razorpay cases differently,
? this is the core user transaction function.
Allows user to cancel own unpaid booking.
? paid bookings cannot be cancelled from self-service,
? protects payment integrity and ensures admin has reason for cancellation.
Shows booking invoice page in browser.
? simple export without external PDF library.
? registration card was removed from template, but `registration_count` is still passed.
That is not harmful, but it is unused context now.
Admin page to add packages and list all packages.
Admin page to manage booking list.
? to enrich bookings with display name/email and approval form.
Shows all feedback entries to admin.
Shows stored signup data to admin.
Even if card is removed from admin home, this route still exists.
? admin needs final control over booking acceptance or rejection.
? loads bookings with related user and package,
? attaches approval form for each row.
? keeps `manage_bookings()` cleaner and avoids repeated template preparation code.
This handles Razorpay redirect/callback after payment.
? payment confirmation should not trust frontend blindly.
Handles server-to-server payment events from Razorpay.
? updates payment status based on event type:
? webhook is more reliable than browser redirect alone.
Logs out current user and redirects home.
This file maps URL paths to functions.
Shows different menu items based on user role:
Full package details plus booking UI and payment area.
Readable invoice screen for a booking.
HTML layout used for invoice download.
Booking management page with status and actions.
The `accounts/static/css/` folder contains page styling split across files such as:
? separation of page-specific and common styles.
? `base.html` currently loads `home.css`, which acts like a compiled or umbrella stylesheet for the site.
This file handles feedback modal behavior.
? improves UX without full page reload.
This file registers models with Django’s built-in admin site.
? allows quick inspection and manual admin operations from Django admin panel.
This file contains automated tests for major project behavior.
? they prove the critical workflows are working.
The `accounts/migrations/` folder stores database schema evolution.
? they track how database structure changed over time.
2. Django `User` and custom `registration` row are created.
7. Booking row is created or updated.
11. User can cancel unpaid booking with reason.
1. Admin logs in through admin portal.
6. Admin approves or rejects pending bookings.
9. Admin can inspect registrations through registrations page.
20. Why Specific Design Choices Were Made
Why use Django `User` plus separate `registration` model?
Why use function-based views instead of class-based views for website pages?
? very explicit for student project and viva explanation,
Why use DRF separately for APIs?
? website and API have different response formats,
? serializers make JSON output clean and validated.
Why store package images in both `image` and `image_url` options?
? admin can either upload files or reuse hosted URLs.
Why use Razorpay callback and webhook both?
? webhook provides reliable server-side payment synchronization.
? user gets immediate feedback after actions like booking, cancelling, approval, deletion.
21. Important Strengths You Can Tell in Viva
? role-based system for users and admin
These are very good to mention in interview because they show maturity.
This is insecure and should be removed.
? keep password only in Django `User` as hashed password.
No separate profile model linked by OneToOneField
Current `registration` model is independent by email.
? use a proper `UserProfile` linked to Django user.
Invoice download is HTML, not PDF
Currently downloadable invoice is HTML attachment.
? add proper PDF generation using WeasyPrint or ReportLab.
Works fine, but official SDK could simplify integration.
? `registration_count` is still passed though registration card was removed.

My project is Dreamland Travels, a Django-based travel booking web application. It allows users to register, login, browse packages, filter trips, book packages, make online payment through Razorpay, cancel eligible bookings, download invoices, and submit feedback. On the admin side, there is a separate admin portal to manage packages, review bookings, approve or reject requests, and monitor feedback. The backend uses Django ORM, forms, templates, and optional Django REST Framework APIs. I also added automated tests for core booking, payment, admin, and feedback workflows.
24. Short Viva Answer: “Why Did You Choose Django?”

I chose Django because it gives built-in authentication, ORM, admin panel, forms, CSRF protection, template rendering, and strong project structure. Since my project required both rapid development and secure handling of bookings and logins, Django was a very suitable framework.
25. Short Viva Answer: “What Is the Most Important File?”

This project helped me understand full-stack web development in a practical way. I worked with database design, authentication, admin workflows, payment integration, form validation, REST APIs, template rendering, testing, and deployment-related settings. If I continue this project further, I would improve security around profile storage, refactor the large views file into smaller modules, and add real PDF invoice export plus a more normalized user profile design.
